channel.title: Savory on zhouyang — The 25th Frame
channel.description: Recent content in Savory on zhouyang — The 25th Frame

item.title: Scallion Oil Noodles — Shanghai's Midnight Alchemy
item.description: It’s 2am. You’ve just gotten off a late conference call with the Redmond office — the kind where you spent forty minutes explaining the same architecture decision three different ways. Your brain is fried. Your stomach is empty. The only place still open is the noodle cart at the end of the lane, run by an old man who’s been there since before your apartment building existed.

item.title: How to Cook Real Lion's Head Meatballs: The Tender Way
item.description: Editor’s Note: This is the step-by-step companion to Lion’s Head Meatballs — The Giant Pork Meatball That Collapses Under a Spoon. If you haven’t read the story behind these glorious spheres of surrender, start there. Then come back here to make them. This guide assumes you’ve already been convinced that hand-chopping matters. If you haven’t, reread the Enemies section of the narrative. We’ll wait.

item.title: Lion's Head Meatballs — The Giant Pork Meatball That Collapses Under a Spoon
item.description: The spoon sinks in and the meatball gives way without resistance. No knife. No sawing. Just the edge of a ceramic soupspoon pressing down and the whole thing — this fist-sized sphere of pork and fat and dissolved tendon — opens like a flower. That’s the first thing you need to understand about hóngshāo shīzi tóu (红烧狮子头). It’s a meatball that’s forgotten how to be solid.
